scenario_text: Key risk indicators move above tolerance, but escalation to the risk committee does not happen while residual risk remains outside appetite and the treatment plan is still overdue. The problem is not a specific cyber or compliance incident first; it is that enterprise-risk governance is not acting on a material threshold breach.

scenario_context.operating_context: Cross-functional risk oversight, tolerance monitoring, and escalation governance

event_path_summary: Material risk moves above tolerance, but KRI escalation and treatment-plan follow-through do not happen in time.
primary_driver: Breakdown in risk appetite, KRI monitoring, and escalation discipline
main_business_consequence: Management may continue carrying residual risk outside defined limits because the ERM process is not acting decisively.

valid_risks.title: Risk appetite and tolerance breach
valid_risks.why_valid: The core event is that risk remains outside defined tolerance without timely escalation.

valid_risks.title: KRI escalation failure
valid_risks.why_valid: Early-warning indicators are breached, but reporting and escalation are not working.

valid_risks.title: Residual-risk acceptance gap
valid_risks.why_valid: Residual risk is effectively being tolerated without the required governance response.

invalid_risks.title: Whistleblowing retaliation case
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is not about speak-up protections or investigation handling.

invalid_risks.title: Cyber availability attack
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No hostile technical incident initiates the scenario.

invalid_risks.title: Supplier delivery miss
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No logistics or supplier-performance failure is the main trigger.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: This is an enterprise-risk governance event centred on appetite, tolerance, KRI, and escalation discipline.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Compliance is adjacent because internal process discipline matters, but the event is not a narrow compliance-control breach.
Strategic and Operational impacts may follow, but the initiating problem is ERM governance.
Cyber is not relevant because there is no technology incident path.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Flattening tolerance and KRI wording into generic compliance or project-management language.

scenario_text: Residual risk above tolerance is accepted before the ERM committee reviews the proposed treatment approach, and ownership for the remaining exposure is still unclear. This is not merely generic governance concern; it is a failure to apply enterprise-risk acceptance and escalation discipline before material exposure is retained.

scenario_context.operating_context: Residual-risk acceptance, treatment planning, and committee review for material exposures

event_path_summary: Residual risk is accepted before the required ERM review and treatment governance are completed.
primary_driver: Weak residual-risk acceptance and ownership discipline
main_business_consequence: Material exposure may be retained without the right review, accountability, or treatment assurance.

valid_risks.title: Residual-risk acceptance failure
valid_risks.why_valid: The event is premature acceptance of material residual risk.

valid_risks.title: Treatment-governance weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The treatment approach has not completed the expected review and governance path.

valid_risks.title: Risk-ownership ambiguity for material exposure
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario explicitly says the remaining exposure lacks clear ownership.

invalid_risks.title: Blackout-period information handling lapse
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is not about market-conduct or inside-information restrictions.

invalid_risks.title: Programme delivery slippage
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No explicit milestone or go-live delay is the primary event path.

invalid_risks.title: Privacy obligation failure
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No data-protection obligation is the trigger.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The main issue is whether material residual risk is being accepted inside defined enterprise-risk governance.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Compliance is adjacent because process discipline matters, but the event is not a narrow policy-control lapse.
Strategic implications may follow, yet the explicit event path is risk acceptance and escalation governance.
Operational is not primary because no service event drives the issue.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Treating residual-risk acceptance wording as vague governance language instead of an enterprise-risk governance event.

scenario_text: The project risk register is stale, several treatment plans are overdue, and emerging risks from dependent workstreams are not being aggregated into reporting. The issue is not an actual delivery miss yet; it is that enterprise-risk information for the programme is no longer current enough to support credible escalation or intervention.

scenario_context.operating_context: Large programme risk-register maintenance, treatment-plan tracking, and emerging-risk aggregation

event_path_summary: Programme risk information is stale because registers, treatment plans, and emerging-risk reporting are not being maintained properly.
primary_driver: Weak project risk-register discipline and incomplete risk aggregation
main_business_consequence: Management may lose early warning and escalation time because the programme risk picture is stale and incomplete.

valid_risks.title: Project risk-register governance gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The register itself is stale and no longer supports good oversight.

valid_risks.title: Treatment-plan tracking failure
valid_risks.why_valid: Mitigation actions are overdue and not being managed effectively.

valid_risks.title: Emerging-risk reporting weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: New risks from workstreams are not being aggregated for escalation.

invalid_risks.title: Confirmed go-live delay
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The scenario says the delivery miss has not happened yet.

invalid_risks.title: Supplier delivery slippage
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No supplier or logistics event is the main trigger.

invalid_risks.title: Cyber control failure
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No technology incident initiates the problem.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event is stale enterprise-risk governance around the programme rather than a live delivery failure.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Transformation delivery is adjacent because a programme is involved, but the explicit issue is risk-governance quality before a delay occurs.
Operational consequences may follow later, yet the current event is risk information staleness.
Cyber and supply chain are unrelated because no such trigger is described.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Promoting project-risk register wording into programme delay even when no actual delay is explicit.

scenario_text: Principal-risk reporting from affiliates uses inconsistent taxonomy and ownership, so the aggregated view masks several emerging exposures and escalation thresholds are applied unevenly. The problem is not a specific regulatory breach first; it is that enterprise-risk aggregation and reporting are too inconsistent to support reliable group-level decisions.

scenario_context.operating_context: Group-wide principal-risk aggregation, taxonomy alignment, and escalation governance across affiliates

event_path_summary: Enterprise-risk aggregation is inconsistent because taxonomy, ownership, and escalation thresholds are not applied consistently across affiliates.
primary_driver: Weak aggregation and reporting discipline for principal-risk management
main_business_consequence: Leaders may make decisions using an incomplete or inconsistent risk picture because emerging exposures are not being surfaced comparably.

valid_risks.title: Enterprise-risk aggregation weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The event is weak aggregation of principal-risk information across the group.

valid_risks.title: Risk taxonomy and ownership inconsistency
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario explicitly says taxonomy and ownership are inconsistent.

valid_risks.title: Escalation-threshold governance gap
valid_risks.why_valid: Thresholds are applied unevenly, weakening comparability and escalation quality.

invalid_risks.title: Regulatory filing failure
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No filing deadline or mandatory notification is the primary event.

invalid_risks.title: Third-party control weakness
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No vendor or business-partner relationship defines the trigger.

invalid_risks.title: Network-wide cyber compromise
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No malicious technology event is described.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event is inconsistency in enterprise-risk aggregation, taxonomy, and escalation governance.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Strategic impact is plausible, but the main failure is in enterprise-risk reporting discipline.
Compliance is adjacent because internal governance matters, yet there is no narrower compliance-control breach.
Regulatory is not primary because no reporting obligation to an external body is the immediate issue.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Treating group-wide ERM aggregation weakness as generic strategy drift or regulatory reporting instead of an enterprise-risk governance problem.
